name: Ernest Hemingway - The Old Man and the Sea
reason: A masterful novella about an aging fisherman's epic struggle with a giant marlin

name: Harper Lee - To Kill a Mockingbird
reason: A poignant novel about racial injustice and moral growth in the American South

name: Toni Morrison - Beloved
reason: A haunting exploration of the enduring trauma of slavery through the story of a runaway mother

name: Cormac McCarthy - The Road
reason: A stark, powerful tale of a father and son surviving in a post-apocalyptic world

name: Junot Díaz - The Brief Wondrous Life of Oscar Wao
reason: A vibrant novel weaving Dominican history with the story of a misfit sci-fi obsessive

name: Anthony Doerr - All the Light We Cannot See
reason: A luminous story of a blind French girl and a German boy whose paths cross in occupied France

name: Colson Whitehead - The Underground Railroad
reason: A daring reimagining of the Underground Railroad as an actual railroad in the antebellum South

name: Colson Whitehead - The Nickel Boys
reason: A devastating novel based on the true story of a brutal reform school in Florida

name: Barbara Kingsolver - Demon Copperhead
reason: A modern retelling of David Copperfield set in contemporary Appalachia

name: Percival Everett - James
reason: A bold retelling of Adventures of Huckleberry Finn from the perspective of Jim

name: Staff of The New York Times
reason: for courageous, groundbreaking coverage of the COVID-19 pandemic

name: Staff of The Minnesota Star Tribune
reason: for coverage of a shooting at a back-to-school Mass that left two children dead and 17 wounded
